question: Tom has 12 apples. Jim has 5 more apples than Tom. Sarah has 3 times as many apples as Jim. How many apples does Sarah have?
Two trains start 300 km apart and move toward each other, one at 40 km/h and the other at 60 km/h. After how many hours do they meet?
A father is 4 times as old as his son. In 20 years, he will be twice as old as his son. How old is the son now?
A machine makes 8 widgets per hour. It runs for 6 hours, but during the last 2 hours it runs 25% slower. How many widgets does it make in total?
An $80 jacket is discounted by 25%, and then a further 10% is taken off the reduced price. What is the final price?
A pool holds 600 liters. One pipe fills it in 30 minutes; another drains it in 60 minutes. With both open, how many minutes does it take to fill from empty?
What is the next number in the sequence 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ...?
What is the remainder when 3^100 is divided by 5?
A bag has 3 red, 4 blue, and 5 green marbles. You draw 2 marbles without replacement. What is the probability that both are green?
A book has 300 pages. Maria reads 20 pages per day for the first 5 days, then 30 pages per day after that. On which day does she finish the book?
3 painters can paint a house in 8 days. Working at the same rate, how many days would 4 painters take?
You deposit $1000 at 10% simple interest per year. What is the total amount after 3 years?
It is 9:00 now. On a 12-hour clock, what time will it be 100 hours later?
A recipe uses flour and sugar in the ratio 5:3. If you use 800 g of flour, how many grams of sugar are needed?
A car travels 60 km at 60 km/h and then another 60 km at 30 km/h. What is its average speed over the whole 120 km trip?
How many squares of all sizes are there on a 3x3 grid of unit squares?
At a party of 6 people, every pair shakes hands exactly once. How many handshakes occur in total?
You mix 3 liters of a 20% salt solution with 2 liters of pure water. What is the salt concentration of the resulting mixture?
You climb a staircase of 5 steps, taking either 1 or 2 steps at a time. How many distinct ways are there to reach the top?
A number is doubled, then 6 is added, then the result is halved, giving 11. What was the original number?
A bacteria colony doubles in size every hour. After 5 hours there are 320 bacteria. How many were there at the start?
What is the least common multiple of 12 and 18?
Pens cost $3 each and notebooks cost $5 each. You buy 4 pens and some notebooks for a total of $37. How many notebooks did you buy?
A 240 km trip is driven in two halves: the first 120 km at 80 km/h and the second 120 km at 60 km/h. How many hours does the whole trip take?
